text:
Souheil (スウヘイル, Sūheiru) was a player of the Greed Island game, as well as a member of Team Kazsule and his alliance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 151 

Appearance

Souheil was a leanly-built young man. He had medium-length hair, which was tied back in a small ponytail. He wore a sleeveless long shirt fastened with a belt, and normal trousers and shoes.

Personality

Not much is known about Souheil's personality, but he did speak in favor of Gon's team being allowed to join the alliance meeting when Asta scorned them for having no use. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 154 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Souheil and Nick Cue are present when Gon trades his "Witch's Love Potion" to Kazsule for their "Book of V.I.P. Passes". 

Souheil and his team get in contact with other Greed Island players, including Gon's team to exchange information on how to stop Genthru's team, the Bombers, from completing the game. Asta, the team leader of her own team, is skeptical that Gon's team will be of any help after hearing that they don't know how ranking works in the game. Souheil, however, argues that they may have information she does not know, but she continues to argue with them. The meeting eventually progresses, and an alliance is formed by the fifteen players who attended the meeting. They decide to get a monopoly on the "Plot of Beach" card, and the alliance heads to Soufrabi to find it. 

After the group arrives in Soufrabi, Kazsule explains how to obtain an SS-rank card. The alliance members split into groups to gather information on the "Plot of Beach" card. After asking around the town, Kazsule finds a female NPC who has information on its location. She agrees to tell the alliance members its location if they drive Razor and his pirates out of town. Amana wonders if fifteen players are a prerequisite to activate the quest for the card, and Souheil questions how it would be possible for an NPC character to recognize this. Killua then deduces that it's by an "Accompany" card. So after Kazsule asks where the pirates are, the alliance goes to a bar in the town where they confront some of the pirates. One of the pirates, Bopobo, challenges the group to a sumo match, promising to take them to Razor if they win. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 155 Zeho steps up to the challenge and fails, so Killua tries and quickly beats the pirate. At the pirates' lighthouse base, Razor challenges the group to a series of sports trials. The first is boxing, and Montreux volunteers to take on the pirate boxer. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 156 

When the match begins, Montreux immediately goes on the offensive and tries to instantly KO the pirate with a flurry of punches, which impresses Souheil and Manheim, but he faints and loses due to the boxer using his ability. Biscuit also throws her match against the pirate footballer, much to Souheil and the others' confusion. The alliance members throw the other matches and are kicked out of Razor's base. Figuring that there's no way for the Bombers to complete the game, the alliance disbands. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 157 

Souheil's name is mentioned in Gon's binder. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 158 After Gon and his team beat Razor and his pirates, it's learned that the Bombers killed Souheil and the remainder of the alliance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 17, Chapter 169 

Abilities & Powers

Gon's team estimated him to be too weak to defeat Razor's convicts, as turned out to be the case. 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Souheil was capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121
